raw_output:
Okay, let’s explore some fantastic meal ideas designed to help you build muscle! Focusing on getting enough protein is key, and there are tons of delicious options to do just that. Here's a breakdown categorized by mealtime with some specific suggestions:

**Breakfast (Setting the Stage)**

* **Greek Yogurt Parfait:** Layer Greek yogurt (a fantastic source of protein) with berries and a sprinkle of granola for sustained energy.  Aim for at least 20-30g of protein in this one.
* **Oatmeal Power Bowl:** Cook oatmeal as usual, then stir in a scoop of whey protein powder (or plant-based alternative if you prefer), chopped nuts, and sliced banana. This is customizable and easy to scale up.
* **Scrambled Eggs with Whole Wheat Toast & Spinach:** A classic for a reason! Eggs are packed with protein, and spinach adds valuable nutrients. You can easily add some feta cheese for extra flavor too. 

**Lunch (Fueling Your Workouts)**

* **Chicken or Turkey Breast Salad Sandwich:** Use whole-grain bread and load it up with sliced chicken breast or turkey breast, mixed greens, tomato, and a light vinaigrette dressing.  (Around 30-40g of protein)
* **Lentil Soup & Whole Grain Roll:** Lentils are an amazing plant-based source of protein and fiber – this will keep you feeling full. (Approximately 18-25g of protein)
* **Quinoa Bowl with Black Beans, Corn & Salsa:** Quinoa is a complete protein! Combine it with seasoned black beans, sweet corn, fresh salsa and avocado for a vibrant and filling bowl. (Around 15-20g of protein)


**Dinner (Recovery & Muscle Repair)**

* **Baked Salmon with Roasted Vegetables:** Salmon is rich in protein and Omega-3 fatty acids which are beneficial for overall health. Pair it with roasted broccoli, sweet potatoes, or Brussels sprouts.  (Approximately 30-45g of protein depending on portion size).
* **Chicken Stir-Fry with Brown Rice:**  A quick and easy option! Use chicken breast cut into bite-sized pieces and stir-fry with plenty of colorful vegetables (peppers, onions, snap peas) in a light sauce. (Around 30-40g protein)
* **Turkey Meatloaf with Mashed Potatoes & Green Beans:** A comforting classic made healthier by using lean ground turkey and loading up on nutrient-rich veggies! (Approx. 35-45g of protein).

**Snacks (Maintaining Protein Levels Throughout the Day)**

* **Cottage Cheese with Fruit:**  Cottage cheese is a fantastic protein boost – about 25g per cup.
* **Hard-Boiled Eggs:** Convenient and portable - about 6g of protein per egg.
* **Protein Shake:** A quick and easy way to get a concentrated dose of protein after your workout (whey or plant-based).



**Important Note:** To really build muscle, you'll also want to focus on getting enough calories overall – slightly more than you burn each day.  Also, don’t underestimate the importance of strength training! 

To help me refine these suggestions further, could you tell me:

*   Are there any particular flavors or cuisines you enjoy? (e.g., Mediterranean, Asian, Mexican?)
